tldr: Meilisearch is a lexical/typo-tolerant search engine; Pinecone is a managed vector database. Migration is not a drop-in swap — you must generate embeddings for each document and rethink ranking. Plan for re-indexing and query-layer rewrites.
why_switch: Need semantic/AI search over keyword matching for RAG and embedding-based retrieval
Want fully managed scaling without operating self-hosted clusters
Mature SDKs and tight integration with LLM/AI workflows
Sub-50ms similarity search across high-dimensional embeddings
Offload index ops, replication, and tuning to a managed service
what_to_export: Document corpus (raw text and metadata used for indexing)
Ranking rules and custom relevancy config
Tenant tokens / API keys mapping for re-issue
Saved queries or analytics baselines for relevance comparison
User/session metadata used for personalization (if any)
export_method: Use Meilisearch's documents endpoint to dump each index as JSON/NDJSON (or take a Meilisearch dump). Persist index settings separately. Embeddings are not stored in Meilisearch by default — you will need to generate them downstream.
import_method: For each document, generate vector embeddings with your chosen model, then upsert vectors plus metadata into a Pinecone index. Recreate filterable fields as Pinecone metadata. Replace lexical queries with embedding-based similarity queries (optionally hybrid with sparse vectors).

concept_mapping.note: Pinecone index is bound to a vector dimension and similarity metric — pick once, hard to change later.
Original text typically lives in metadata; the vector replaces the searchable body.
Decide which fields to concatenate before embedding; there is no per-field weighting at query time.
Keep types simple (string, number, bool, list) for filter compatibility.
Lexical relevancy and typo tolerance do not map directly; consider hybrid search or a re-ranker.
Embeddings absorb most synonym handling; explicit synonym lists are not a Pinecone concept.
Pinecone is not an aggregation engine — facet counts must be precomputed or done elsewhere.
Use namespaces per tenant for isolation within a single index.

effort: 1-3 weeks for a small/medium corpus; longer if relevance tuning or hybrid search is required
gotchas: Paradigm shift: lexical to vector — relevance behavior will differ and needs evaluation
Embedding cost and latency are now part of every write and query
Pinecone metadata filters have size and type constraints; large text fields should not live in metadata
Index dimension and metric are fixed at creation — choose the embedding model first
Faceting, typo tolerance, and ranking rules from Meilisearch have no direct equivalent
Re-indexing the full corpus is required; budget for embedding generation throughput

email.subject: Moving search from Meilisearch to Pinecone
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning to migrate our search stack from Meilisearch to Pinecone to support semantic/AI search and offload ops to a managed service. This is not a like-for-like swap: Meilisearch is lexical, Pinecone is a vector database, so we'll re-index the corpus through an embedding model and rewrite the query layer.

Plan: export documents and index settings from Meilisearch, pick an embedding model and dimension, create the Pinecone index, upsert vectors with metadata, and validate relevance against a saved query set before cutover. Expect 1-3 weeks depending on corpus size and relevance tuning.

Let me know concerns or constraints before we start.
